reason: Load current frontend contract before refactoring UI and performance.
Keep route, feature, component, and shared primitive placement consistent.
Apply component, accessibility, focus, and interaction state rules during UI modernization.
Avoid unstable effects/selectors and keep browser-side behavior in appropriate hooks/components.
Preserve upload/history/admin/storage option contracts while touching frontend data flow.
Run required frontend checks and avoid duplicated request logic.
Prefer shared primitives and tokens over page-local one-off styling.
Ensure UI refactor does not break frontend/backend upload, storage, admin, or history contracts.
Use the task-specific UI/performance scope and visual thesis.
